evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Adopt best practices in data modeling, ensuring data integrity, creating resilient test designs, and developing comprehensive documentation.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Responsibilities emphasise data integrity, resilient testing, documentation, and quality/observability with no velocity or speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: ensure the timely and accurate delivery of financial reports
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The role explicitly owns financial reporting accuracy and reconciliation at a real-money gaming/betting company, meeting the high domain-risk bar via explicit financial reporting language.
evidence.data_team_maturity: working on the silver and gold layers of our medallion architecture
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: A named, established medallion architecture with dedicated layers (silver/gold) and four core specialised pillars (discoverability, security, quality, observability) at a 2,700-person company indicates a mature, specialised data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: working on the silver and gold layers of our medallion architecture
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: The medallion architecture and layer-specific framing is technically precise, but several bullets ("Support stakeholders", "Translate complex business and functional requirements") are generic boilerplate, giving a mix of both.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support stakeholders by applying reporting and analysis processes to ensure the timely and accurate delivery of financial reports.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The first and most concrete responsibility is financial reporting and reconciliation, placing the primary audience in finance/executive reporting.
evidence.autonomy_level: Support stakeholders by applying reporting and analysis processes to ensure the timely and accurate delivery of financial reports.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Verbs like "support", "enhance", "translate", and "adopt best practices" show the role executes and delivers within an existing architecture rather than setting new direction.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools or AI-consuming infrastructure appears anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Adopt best practices in data modeling, ensuring data integrity, creating resilient test designs, and developing comprehensive documentation.
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Test design and data integrity are named as an owned activity with a clear action verb ("creating resilient test designs"), not merely a tool in a requirements list.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: ensure the timely and accurate delivery of financial reports
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Financial reporting accuracy and reconciliation appear in the first responsibility and role summary, and quality/security/observability language repeats throughout, matching high loss-aversion framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Enhance reporting and analysis processes by automating workflows or simplifying self-service modeling for stakeholders.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The role enhances and automates existing reporting/analysis processes within an established architecture rather than building from zero, so fix/scale.
evidence.loss_aversion: ensure the timely and accurate delivery of financial reports — reflects concern about bad financial data reaching decision-makers, a trust/compliance-adjacent fear rather than just pipeline reliability.
